generatedSentence: Elara, the owner of a struggling digital art studio, often found her creative calling pulling her away from the mundane. Even after a long day connected to the internet, she'd unwind by biking through the twilight, her favorite jackets keeping her warm. Tonight, she peeled a ripe apricot, the sweet scent momentarily distracting her from the complexities of her business, before changing into a simple nightgown for bed. A series of sophisticated hacks had targeted her company, exposing fraudulent claims made by a former partner. The situation was getting nasty, threatening to lay waste to her reputation. Her website was overgrown with digital weeds, and a new data thicket made it impossible to trace the origin. It felt like an attack on her entire digital biome. She knew something was deeply wrong. "Ouch," she muttered, having tripped over a loose cable in her dimly lit office, the screen's low opacity making it hard to see the new blocker on her server. She slammed a wok in frustration, the clang echoing. The digital assault intensified, turning her business into a tangled welter of compromised data. A vision of a burning longship on a stormy sea filled her mind, symbolizing the destruction. The attackers, fueled by a perverse ideology, seemed intent on a digital eugenics, purging any competitor they deemed inferior. Their tactics felt almost voluptuous in their malice, hooking deep into her company's entrails. The sheer obesity of the data breach was overwhelming, leaving her feeling bloated with despair. It threatened to drown her ink-stained dreams, yet her aerodynamics in navigating the digital storm remained. Just as despair threatened to consume her, an anonymous tip, like a digital guardian angel, appeared, offering a crucial lead. After days of relentless work, she finally pinpointed the source. With the crisis averted, she took a well-deserved lunch, enjoying the simple pleasure of the afternoon. Her studio, though battered, was recovering. She looked out at the tranquil mangrove trees, a symbol of resilience and growth. The experience, while harrowing, had reignited her passion, reminding her that true playtime was found in overcoming challenges and creating anew.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, noir comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A determined woman in her late 40s with sharp eyes, a few silver streaks in her dark, swept-back hair, and a concentrated expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara, a woman in her late 40s with silver streaks in her dark hair, biking along a scenic path at twilight, looking contemplative, with a subtle glow of a screen reflected in her eyes, wearing a light jacket, medium shot, slightly low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara, looking stressed, standing amidst multiple computer monitors displaying chaotic error messages and abstract digital 'weeds,' one hand on her forehead in frustration, a wok visibly overturned on a desk beside her, wide shot, slightly high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara's face is etched with despair and determination, the reflection of a fiery, Viking-style longship visible in her pupils, surrounded by abstract, chaotic digital patterns representing data overload and threats, extreme close-up on her face, emphasizing her eyes and the reflections. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara sitting at an outdoor cafe table, enjoying a quiet lunch, a serene, mature smile on her face, with lush mangrove trees visible in the soft background, medium shot, eye-level. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Excess of material, useless by-products or damaged, unsaleable products; garbage; rubbish.
Excrement or urine.
definitions.example: The cage was littered with animal waste

definitions.definition: A waste land; an uninhabited desolate region; a wilderness or desert.
A place that has been laid waste or destroyed.
A large tract of uncultivated land.
The part of the land of a manor (of whatever size) not used for cultivation or grazing, nowadays treated as common land.
A vast expanse of water.
A disused mine or part of one.
The action or progress of wasting; extravagant consumption or ineffectual use.
definitions.example: Her life seemed a waste

definitions.definition: Large abundance of something, specifically without it being used.
Gradual loss or decay.
A decaying of the body by disease; wasting away.
Destruction or devastation caused by war or natural disasters; See "to lay waste"

A cause of action which may be brought by the owner of a future interest in property against the current owner of that property to prevent the current owner from degrading the value or character of the property, either intentionally or through neglect.
Material derived by mechanical and chemical erosion from the land, carried by streams to the sea.
Any plant regarded as unwanted at the place where, and at the time when it is growing.
definitions.example: If it isn't in a straight line or marked with a label, it's a weed.

definitions.definition: Short for duckweed.
Underbrush; low shrubs.
A drug or the like made from the leaves of a plant.
A weak horse, which is therefore unfit to breed from.
A puny person; one who has little physical strength.
Something unprofitable or troublesome; anything useless.
To remove unwanted vegetation from a cultivated area.
definitions.example: I weeded my flower bed.

definitions.definition: A garment or piece of clothing.
Clothing collectively; clothes, dress.
An article of dress worn in token of grief; a mourning garment or badge.
definitions.example: He wore a weed on his hat.

definitions.definition: (especially in the plural as "widow's weeds") (Female) mourning apparel.
A sudden illness or relapse, often attended with fever, which befalls those who are about to give birth, are giving birth, or have recently given birth or miscarried or aborted.
Lymphangitis in a horse.
A pigment (or dye)-based fluid used for writing, printing etc.
A particular type, color or container of this fluid.
The black or dark-colored fluid ejected by squid, octopus etc, as a protective strategy.
Publicity.
definitions.example: The TSA has been getting a lot of ink lately.

definitions.definition: Tattoo work.
Cheap red wine.
To apply ink to; to cover or smear with ink.
To sign (a contract or similar document).
To apply a tattoo to (someone).
(of a squid or octopus) to eject ink (sense 3)
Any set of computer networks that communicate using the Internet Protocol. (An intranet.)
The Internet, the largest global internet.
An internet connection, internet connectivity, access to the internet.
definitions.example: Do you have internet at your place?  My internet is down and I want to check my email.

definitions.definition: A fictitious unit of scoring, awarded for making outstanding posts.
To use the Internet; to search for something using the Internet; to surf the Internet.
definitions.example: Having no idea what that means, I am internetting like mad.

definitions.definition: One who owns something.
definitions.example: The police recovered the stolen car and returned it to its owner.

definitions.definition: The captain of a ship.
Something nasty.
definitions.example: Processed foods are full of aspartame and other nasties.

definitions.definition: (preceded by "the") Sexual intercourse.
A video nasty.
Dirty, filthy.
Contemptible, unpleasant (of a person).
Objectionable, unpleasant (of a thing); repellent, offensive.
Indecent or offensive; obscene, lewd.
Spiteful, unkind.
Awkward, difficult to navigate; dangerous.
Grave or dangerous (of an accident, illness etc.).
Formidable, terrific; wicked.
A long loose robe worn mainly by women for sleeping in.
A dressing gown.
To fall over or stumble over an object as a result of striking it with one's foot
definitions.example: Be careful not to trip on the tree roots.

definitions.definition: (sometimes followed by "up") to cause (a person or animal) to fall or stumble by knocking their feet from under them
definitions.example: A pedestrian was able to trip the burglar as he was running away.

definitions.definition: To be guilty of a misstep or mistake; to commit an offence against morality, propriety, etc
To detect in a misstep; to catch; to convict
To activate or set in motion, as in the activation of a trap, explosive, or switch
definitions.example: When we get into the factory, trip the lights.

definitions.definition: To be activated, as by a signal or an event
definitions.example: The alarm system tripped, throwing everyone into a panic.

definitions.definition: To experience a state of reverie or to hallucinate, due to consuming psychoactive drugs
definitions.example: After taking the LSD, I started tripping about fairies and colors.

definitions.definition: To journey, to make a trip
definitions.example: Last summer we tripped to the coast.

definitions.definition: To move with light, quick steps; to walk or move lightly; to skip
To raise (an anchor) from the bottom, by its cable or buoy rope, so that it hangs free
To pull (a yard) into a perpendicular position for lowering it
(most commonly used in the form tripping) to become unreasonably upset, especially over something unimportant; to cause a scene or a disruption
Any major regional biological community such as that of forest or desert
All the genomes of such a community
To ride a bike.
definitions.example: I biked so much yesterday that I'm very sore today.

definitions.definition: To travel by bike.
definitions.example: It was such a nice day I decided to bike to the store, though it's far enough I usually take my car.

definitions.definition: To transport by bicycle
The sport of riding a bicycle.
definitions.example: Professional biking is plagued with controversies over drug use.

definitions.definition: The practice of riding a motorcycle.
A round sweet and juicy stone fruit, resembling peach or plum in taste, with a yellow-orange flesh, lightly fuzzy skin and a large seed inside.
The apricot tree, Prunus armeniaca
A pale yellow-orange colour, like that of an apricot fruit.
A dog with an orange-coloured coat.
The junction of the brain and brain stem on a target, used as an aiming point to ensure a one-shot kill.
(usually in the plural) A testicle.
Of a pale yellowish-orange colour, like that of an apricot.
A tool for chopping.
A hacking blow.
A gouge or notch made by such a blow.
A dry cough.
A hacking; a catch in speaking; a short, broken cough.
A try, an attempt.
The foothold traditionally cut into the ice from which the person who throws the rock pushes off for delivery.
A mattock or a miner's pickaxe.
An expedient, temporary solution, such as a small patch or change to code, meant to be replaced with a more elegant solution at a later date.
An interesting technical achievement, particularly in computer programming.
A trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: Putting your phone in a sandwich bag when you go to the beach is such a great hack.

definitions.definition: An illegal attempt to gain access to a computer network.
A video game or any computer software that has been altered from its original state.
Time check.
A swing of the bat at a pitched ball by the batter.
definitions.example: He took a few hacks, but the pitcher finally struck him out.

definitions.definition: A kick on the shins in football.
Confinement of an officer to their stateroom as a punishment.
To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
A board which the falcon's food is placed on; used by extension for the state of partial freedom in which they are kept before being trained.
A food-rack for cattle.
A rack used to dry something, such as bricks, fish, or cheese.
A grating in a mill race.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
A horse for hire, especially one which is old and tired.
A person, often a journalist, hired to do routine work.
definitions.example: I got by on hack work for years before I finally published my novel.

definitions.definition: Someone who is available for hire; hireling, mercenary.
A taxicab (hackney cab) driver.
A vehicle let for hire; originally, a hackney coach, now typically a taxicab.
A hearse.
(authorship) An untalented writer.

definitions.definition: One who is professionally successful despite producing mediocre work. (Usually applied to persons in a creative field.)
A talented writer-for-hire, paid to put others' thoughts into felicitous language.
A political agitator. (slightly derogatory)
A writer who hires himself out for any sort of literary work; an overworked man; a drudge.
A procuress.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).
To be exposed or offered or to common use for hire; to turn prostitute.

To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.

To play hackeysack.
Suggestive of or characterized by full, generous, pleasurable sensation.

Applied to an action whose name is forgotten by, unknown or unimportant to the user, e.g. from words of a song.
Having a characteristic that the speaker cannot specify.
(degree) Somewhat; to a degree.
definitions.example: The baby looks something like his father.

definitions.definition: (degree) To a high degree.

definitions.definition: (of someone or something) A quality to a moderate degree.

definitions.example: She has a certain something.

definitions.definition: (often with really or quite) Somebody who or something that is superlative or notable in some way.
definitions.example:
- Some marmosets are less than six inches tall.
- Well, isn't that something?

definitions.definition: A social philosophy or practice which advocates the improvement of human hereditary qualities through selective breeding, either by encouraging people with good genetic qualities to reproduce (positive eugenics), or discouraging people with bad genetic qualities from reproducing (negative eugenics), or by technological means.
A large, oriental, round-bottomed cooking pan.
To prepare oriental cuisine using a wok.
The state or quality of being opaque, not allowing light to pass through
definitions.example: We could not see the sandbar due to the opacity of the muddy water.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being inaccessible to understanding.
definitions.example: The opacity of these decisions is troubling.

definitions.definition: A measure of relative impenetrability to electromagnetic radiation such as light.
definitions.example: The opacity of a clean glass window is near zero.

definitions.definition: An expression of one's own physical pain.
definitions.example: Ouch! You stepped on my toe! That hurt!

definitions.definition: An expression in sympathy at another's pain.
definitions.example: Ouch! Her sunburn looks awful.

definitions.definition: A reply to an insult seen as savage (frequently one that is tongue-in-cheek or joking).
definitions.example: Ouch. How could you say that?

definitions.definition: An expression of disappointment.
definitions.example: Ouch, I really wanted to do that.

definitions.definition: Expressing surprise at the high price of something.
definitions.example: Ouch, one hundred thousand dollars for a car! I could never afford that!

definitions.definition: A dense, but generally small, growth of shrubs, bushes or small trees; a copse.
A dense aggregation of other things, concrete or abstract.
definitions.example: He had to complete a thicket of paperwork before he was allowed to join the company.

definitions.definition: The collection of many small linked files created when a document is saved in HTML format by some word processors and web site creation software.
A tutelary angel watching over a particular person in monotheistic contexts.
A person who watches over another.
A person directly involved in a creative marketing process.

definitions.definition: Artistic material used in advertising, e.g. photographs, drawings, or video.
definitions.example: I've included in my portfolio all the creative I've completed in my five year design career.

definitions.definition: Tending to create things, or having the ability to create; often, excellently, in a novel fashion, or any or all of these.
definitions.example: a creative dramatist who avoids cliche

definitions.definition: (of a created thing) Original, expressive and imaginative.
definitions.example: a creative new solution to an old problem

definitions.definition: A type of set of natural numbers, related to mathematical logic.

definitions.definition: Designed or executed to deceive or mislead.

A job or occupation.

definitions.definition: Swollen with fluid or gas.
Excessively or extremely large or wealthy.

Agent noun of block; something that blocks something else.

To attach a hook to.

definitions.definition: To ensnare or obligate someone, as if with a hook.
definitions.example: A free trial is a good way to hook customers.

definitions.definition: To steal.
To connect (hook into, hook together).
definitions.example: If you hook your network cable into the jack, you'll be on the network.

definitions.definition: (usually in passive) To make addicted; to captivate.
definitions.example: He had gotten hooked on cigarettes in his youth.

definitions.definition: To play a hook shot.

definitions.example: The teacher kept a watchful eye on her pupils during the school trip.

definitions.example: We'd like to thank all the contributors who have invested countless hours into this event.

Any word used as the key to a code.

A reserved word used to identify a specific command, function etc.

One crowned with laurel, such as a poet laureate or Nobel laureate.
A graduate of a university.
To honor with a wreath of laurel, as formerly was done in bestowing a degree at English universities.
(sometimes postpositive) Crowned, or decked, with laurel.
The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.
definitions.example: That year, a meteor fell from the sky.

definitions.definition: The part of the sky which can be seen from a specific place or at a specific time; its condition, climate etc.
definitions.example: I lay back under a warm Texas sky.

definitions.definition: Heaven.
definitions.example: This mortal has incurred the wrath of the skies.

definitions.definition: (theoretical physics) The set of all lightlike lines (or directions) passing through a given point in space-time.
In an art gallery, the upper rows of pictures that cannot easily be seen.
A cloud.
To hit, kick or throw (a ball) extremely high.
To clear (a hurdle, high jump bar, etc.) by a large margin.
To hang (a picture on exhibition) near the top of a wall, where it cannot be well seen.
To drink something from a container without one's lips touching the container.
A vibrant light blue colour, like that of the sky on a fine day.
An athletic supporter worn by men to support the genitals during strenuous exercise.
To engage in political activity; politick.
A methodology and activities associated with running a government, an organization, or a movement.
The profession of conducting political affairs.
definitions.example: He made a career out of politics.

definitions.definition: (plural) One's political stands and opinions.
definitions.example: Their politics are clear from the bumper stickers on their cars.

definitions.definition: Political maneuvers or diplomacy between people, groups, or organizations, especially involving power, influence or conflict.
(singular) Real-world beliefs and social issues irrelevant to the topic at hand.
definitions.example: We're trying to talk about comic books, don't mention politics.

definitions.definition: Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: To ban a person from a city for five or ten years through the procedure of ostracism.
(by extension) To exclude a person from a community or from society by not communicating with them or by refusing to acknowledge their presence; to refuse to associate with or talk to; to shun.
A scarf that is worn looped or tied around the neck.
definitions.example: The Boy Scout wore a red neckerchief, the ends clasped with a sliding knot ornament.

definitions.definition: Make sad or dispirited.
To cast down.
A large, semi-aquatic, herbivorous (plant-eating) African mammal (Hippopotamus amphibius)
The meat of a deer.
definitions.example: After shooting a deer, field dressing is the next step necessary for high quality venison.

definitions.definition: The meat of an antelope.
definitions.example: 2007, Gregory Simon Bull, Marketing fresh venison in the Eastern Cape Province using a niche marketing strategy (thesis), page xcix

definitions.definition: The meat of any wild animal that has been hunted rather than raised domestically.
Firmly or securely fixed in place; stable.
definitions.example: That rope is dangerously loose. Make it fast!

definitions.definition: Firm against attack; fortified by nature or art; impregnable; strong.
(of people) Steadfast, with unwavering feeling. (Now mostly in set phrases like fast friend(s).)
Moving with great speed, or capable of doing so; swift, rapid.
definitions.example: I am going to buy a fast car.

definitions.definition: Causing unusual rapidity of play or action.
definitions.example: a fast billiard table

definitions.definition: (of a piece of hardware) Able to transfer data in a short period of time.
Deep or sound (of sleep); fast asleep (of people).
(of dyes or colours) Not running or fading when subjected to detrimental conditions such as wetness or intense light; permanent.
definitions.example: All the washing has come out pink. That red tee-shirt was not fast.

definitions.definition: Tenacious; retentive.
Having an extravagant lifestyle or immoral habits.
definitions.example: a fast woman

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: There must be something wrong with the hall clock. It is always fast.

definitions.definition: (of photographic film) More sensitive to light than average.
In a firm or secure manner, securely; in such a way as not to be moved; safe, sound .
definitions.example: Hold this rope as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: (of sleeping) Deeply or soundly .
definitions.example: He is fast asleep.

definitions.definition: Immediately following in place or time; close, very near .
definitions.example: Fast by the sturdy batsman the ball unheeded sped. / That ain't my style, said Casey. Strike one, the umpire said.

definitions.definition: Quickly, with great speed; within a short time .
definitions.example: Do it as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: I think my watch is running fast.

definitions.definition: To restrict one’s personal consumption, generally of food, but sometimes other things, in various manners (totally, temporally, by avoiding particular items), often for religious or medical reasons.
definitions.example: Muslims fast during Ramadan and Catholics during Lent.

definitions.definition: A person who frees or liberates.
Violent uncontrolled anger.
A current fashion or fad.
definitions.example: Miniskirts were all the rage back then.

definitions.definition: Any vehement passion.
To act or speak in heightened anger.
(sometimes figurative) To move with great violence, as a storm etc.
To enrage.
To feel itchy; to feel a need to be scratched.
To have a constant, teasing urge; to feel strongly motivated; to want or desire something.
definitions.example: He started learning to drive and he has been itching for opportunities to practice ever since.

definitions.definition: To cause to feel an itch.
To scratch or rub so as to relieve an itch.
A temporary suspension of operation, especially of electrical power supply.
The amount of something lost in storage or transportation.
A writing consisting of multiple glyphs, characters, symbols or sentences.
A book, tome or other set of writings.
A brief written message transmitted between mobile phones.
Data which can be interpreted as human-readable text.
A verse or passage of Scripture, especially one chosen as the subject of a sermon, or in proof of a doctrine.
(by extension) Anything chosen as the subject of an argument, literary composition, etc.
A style of writing in large characters; also, a kind of type used in printing.
definitions.example: German text

definitions.definition: To send a text message to; i.e. to transmit text using the Short Message Service (SMS), or a similar service, between communications devices, particularly mobile phones.
definitions.example: I'll text the address to you as soon as I find it.

definitions.definition: To send and receive text messages.
definitions.example: Have you been texting all afternoon?

definitions.definition: To write in large characters, as in text hand.

A man of gentle but not noble birth, particularly a man of means (originally ownership of property) who does not work for a living but has no official status in a peerage; an armiferous man ranking below a knight.
definitions.example: Being a gentleman, Robert was entitled to shove other commoners into the gongpit but he still had to jump out of the way of the knights to avoid the same fate himself.

definitions.definition: Any well-bred, well-mannered, or charming man.
An effeminate or oversophisticated man.
definitions.example: Well, la-di-da, aren't you just a proper gentleman?

definitions.definition: (polite term of address) Any man.
definitions.example: Please escort this gentleman to the gentlemen's room.

definitions.definition: (usually historical, sometimes derogatory) An amateur or dabbler in any field, particularly those of independent means.
An amateur player, particularly one whose wealth permits him to forego payment.
The ability to hold, receive or absorb
A measure of such ability; volume
The maximum amount that can be held
definitions.example: It was hauling a capacity load.

definitions.definition: Capability; the ability to perform some task
The maximum that can be produced.
Mental ability; the power to learn
A faculty; the potential for growth and development
A role; the position in which one functions
Legal authority (to make an arrest for example)
Electrical capacitance.
(operations) The maximum that can be produced on a machine or in a facility or group.
definitions.example: Its capacity rating was 150 tons per hour, but its actual maximum capacity was 200 tons per hour.

definitions.definition: Filling the allotted space.
definitions.example: There will be a capacity crowd at Busch stadium for the sixth game.

definitions.definition: A sketch or account of anything in words; a portraiture or representation in language; an enumeration of the essential qualities of a thing or species.
The act of describing; a delineation by marks or signs.
A set of characteristics by which someone or something can be recognized.
definitions.example: The zoo had no lions, tigers, or cats of any description.

definitions.definition: A scientific documentation of a taxon for the purpose of introducing it to science.
definitions.example: The type description of the fungus was written by a botanist.

definitions.definition: The act or practice of recording and describing actual language usage in a given speech community, as opposed to prescription, i.e. laying down norms of language usage.
A descriptive linguistic survey.
A failure, particularly mechanical; something that has failed
definitions.example: We saw a breakdown by the side of the road.

definitions.definition: A physical collapse or lapse of mental stability
definitions.example: After so much stress, he suffered a breakdown and simply gave up.

definitions.definition: Listing, division or categorization in great detail
definitions.example: Looking at the breakdown of the budget, I see a few items we could cut.

definitions.definition: A detailed description of a forthcoming project, including the characters and roles required.
Breaking of chemical bonds within a compound to produce simpler compounds or elements.
A musical technique by which the music is stripped down, becoming simpler, varying in heaviness depending on the genre.
A loss of organization (of the parts of a system).
A noisy, rapid, shuffling dance engaged in competitively by a number of persons or pairs in succession, common in Southern United States African American music.
Any crude, noisy dance performed by shuffling the feet, usually by one person at a time.
Any rapid bluegrass dance tune, especially featuring a five-string banjo.
definitions.example: "Foggy Mountain Breakdown"

definitions.definition: The percussion break of songs chosen by a DJ for use in hip-hop music.
The exterior body of a motor vehicle.
The repair of a such body.
The application of physical therapy as a preventive measure.
A service offered that includes an erotic massage and may also include prostitution.
A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
A suite of rooms within a domicile, designated for a specific person or persons and including a bedroom.
A division of an enclosure that is separate from others; a compartment
(COM) A conceptual space used for separation in the threading architecture. Objects in one apartment cannot directly access those in another, but must use a proxy.
A two-handled jar with a narrow neck that was used in ancient times to store or carry wine or oil.
One of various units for measuring liquid or volume during the Roman Empire, measuring between 18.5 and 39 litres depending on the variant.
Ancient unit of volume, for the measurement of the internal capacity of a ship.
In botany, the lower valve of the fruit that opens transversely.
A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.
definitions.example: Today is a Wiccan holiday!

definitions.definition: A day declared free from work by the state or government.
A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).
definitions.example: I want to take a French course this summer holiday.

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.
definitions.example: a mortgage payment holiday

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
One who enters into, or offers for, any service of his/her own free will, especially when done without pay.

One who enters into military service voluntarily, but who, when in service, is subject to discipline and regulations like other soldiers; -- opposed to conscript; specifically, a voluntary member of the organized militia of a country as distinguished from the standing army.
A person who acts out of his own will without a legal obligation, such as a donor.
A plant that grows spontaneously, without being cultivated on purpose; see volunteer plant in Wikipedia.
A native or resident of the American state of Tennessee.
To enlist oneself as a volunteer.
To do or offer to do something voluntarily.
definitions.example: to volunteer for doing the dishes

definitions.definition: To offer, usually unprompted.
definitions.example: to volunteer an explanation

definitions.definition: To grow without human sowing or intentional cultivation.
To offer the services of (someone else) to do something.
definitions.example: My sister volunteered me to do the dishes.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation of size between a hamlet and a town.
definitions.example: There are 2 churches and 3 shops in our village.

definitions.definition: A rural habitation that has a church, but no market.
A planned community such as a retirement community or shopping district.
A gated community.
A wish of happiness or safety at parting, especially a permanent departure
A departure; the act of leaving
To bid farewell or say goodbye.
Parting, valedictory, final.
definitions.example: a farewell discourse;  the band's farewell tour

definitions.definition: Goodbye.
definitions.example: He said "Farewell!" and left.

definitions.definition: A flash of light produced by short-duration, high-voltage discharge of electricity within a cloud, between clouds, or between a cloud and the earth.
definitions.example: Although we did not see the lightning, we did hear the thunder.

definitions.definition: A discharge of this kind.
definitions.example: That tree was hit by lightning.

definitions.definition: Anything that moves very fast.
The act of making bright, or the state of being made bright; enlightenment; brightening, as of the mental powers.
To produce lightning.
Extremely fast or sudden; moving (as if) at the speed of lightning.
Someone who is an expert in, or devoted to, some specific branch of study or research.
A physician whose practice is limited to a particular branch of medicine or surgery.
Any of several non-commissioned ranks corresponding to that of corporal.
An organism that is specialized for a particular environment.
definitions.example: The tree Lepidothamnus laxifolius is a high alpine specialist found in high-altitude bog communities and in scrub.

definitions.definition: Specialised.
A seat (tack) for a rider placed on the back of a horse or other animal.
An item of harness (harness saddle) placed on the back of a horse or other animal.
A seat on a bicycle, motorcycle, etc.
A cut of meat that includes both loins and part of the backbone.
A low point, in the shape of a saddle, between two hills.
A formation of gold-bearing quartz occurring along the crest of an anticlinal fold, especially in Australia.
The raised floorboard in a doorway.
A small tapered or sloped area structure that helps channel surface water to drains.
A block of wood, usually fastened to one spar and shaped to receive the end of another.
A part, such as a flange, which is hollowed out to fit upon a convex surface and serve as a means of attachment or support.
The clitellum of an earthworm.
Any of the saddle-like markings on a boa constrictor.
A saddle shoe.
The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.
An effort; an attempt; a venture.
A dislocation in a stratum; a fault.
An abrupt interruption of level in a piece of brickwork or masonry.
An instance of propelling oneself upwards.
definitions.example: The boy took a skip and a jump down the lane.

definitions.definition: An object which causes one to jump, a ramp.
definitions.example: He went off a jump.

definitions.definition: An instance of causing oneself to fall from an elevated location.
definitions.example: There were a couple of jumps from the bridge.

definitions.definition: An instance of employing a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
definitions.example: She was terrified before the jump, but was thrilled to be skydiving.

definitions.definition: An instance of reacting to a sudden stimulus by jerking the body.
A jumping move in a board game.
definitions.example: the knight's jump in chess

definitions.definition: A button (of a joypad, joystick or similar device) used to make a video game character jump (propel itself upwards).
definitions.example: Press jump to start.

definitions.definition: An obstacle that forms part of a showjumping course, and that the horse has to jump over cleanly.
definitions.example: Heartless managed the scale the first jump but fell over the second.

definitions.definition: (with on) An early start or an advantage.
definitions.example: He got a jump on the day because he had laid out everything the night before.

definitions.definition: A discontinuity in the graph of a function, where the function is continuous in a punctured interval of the discontinuity.
An instance of faster-than-light travel, not observable from ordinary space.
A change of the path of execution to a different location.
Short for jump-start.
definitions.example: My car won't start. Could you give me a jump?

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
Exact; matched; fitting; precise.
Exactly; precisely
An occasion when a performer or team of them (especially in vaudeville) expects to perform at a theater for a single evening.

A single sexual encounter between two individuals, where at least one of the partners has no immediate intention or expectation of establishing a longer-term sexual or romantic relationship. As the phrase implies, the relationship lasts for only one night.
Either of the two partners involved in such a single sexual encounter.
An action or statement in response to a stimulus or other event.
definitions.example: The announcement of the verdict brought a violent reaction.

definitions.definition: A transformation in which one or more substances is converted into another by combination or decomposition.
definitions.example: In this reaction, the acid and base will neutralize each other, producing a salt.

definitions.definition: Reactionary politics; a period in which reactionary thought or politics is resurgent or dominant.
A periodically sent publication containing current events or the like, generally on a particular topic or geared toward a limited audience.
A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.
definitions.example: a block of ice

definitions.definition: A chopping block; cuboid base for cutting or beheading.
definitions.example: Anne Boleyn placed her head on the block and awaited her execution.

definitions.definition: A group of urban lots of property, several acres in extent, not crossed by public streets.
definitions.example: I'm going for a walk around the block.

definitions.definition: A residential building consisting of flats.
definitions.example: a block of flats

definitions.definition: The distance from one street to another in a city that is built (approximately) to a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The place you are looking for is two long blocks east and one short block north.

definitions.definition: Interference or obstruction of cognitive processes.
The human head.
definitions.example: I'll knock your block off!

definitions.definition: A wig block: a simplified head model upon which wigs are worn.
A mould on which hats, bonnets, etc., are shaped.
A set of sheets (of paper) joined together at one end.
definitions.example: a block of 100 tickets

definitions.definition: A logical data storage unit containing one or more physical sectors (see cluster).
A region of code in a program that acts as a single unit, such as a function or loop.
A fixed-length group of bits making up part of a message.
A case with one or more sheaves/pulleys, used with ropes to increase or redirect force, for example, as part of the rigging of a sailing ship.
A portion of a macromolecule, comprising many units, that has at least one feature not present in adjacent portions.
Something that prevents something from passing.
definitions.example: There's a block in the pipe that means the water can't get through.

definitions.definition: An action to interfere with the movement of an opposing player or of the object of play (ball, puck).
A shot played by holding the bat vertically in the path of the ball, so that it loses momentum and drops to the ground.
A defensive play by one or more players meant to deflect a spiked ball back to the hitter’s court.
A joined group of four (or in some cases nine) postage stamps, forming a roughly square shape.
A section of split logs used as fuel.
Solitary confinement.
A cellblock.
The perch on which a bird of prey is kept.
A piece of hard wood on which a stereotype or electrotype plate is mounted.
A blockhead; a stupid fellow; a dolt.
A section of a railroad where the block system is used.
The position of a player or bat when guarding the wicket.
A blockhole.
The popping crease.
A discrete group of vines in a vineyard, often distinguished from others by variety, clone, canopy training method, irrigation infrastructure, or some combination thereof.
To fill (something) so that it is not possible to pass.
definitions.example: The pipe is blocked.

definitions.definition: To prevent (something or someone) from passing.
definitions.example: You're blocking the road – I can't get through!

definitions.definition: To prevent (something from happening or someone from doing something).
definitions.example: His plan to take over the business was blocked by the boss.

definitions.definition: To impede an opponent.
definitions.example: He blocked the basketball player's shot.

definitions.definition: To specify the positions and movements of the actors.
definitions.example: It was very difficult to block this scene convincingly.

definitions.definition: To hit with a block.
To play a block shot.
To disable communication via telephone, instant messaging, etc., with an undesirable someone.
definitions.example: I tried to send you a message, but you've blocked me!

definitions.definition: To wait.
definitions.example: When the condition expression is false, the thread blocks on the condition variable.

definitions.definition: To stretch or mould (a knitted item, a hat, etc.) into the desired shape.
definitions.example: I blocked the mittens by wetting them and pinning them to a shaped piece of cardboard.

definitions.definition: To shape or sketch out roughly.
To inspire boredom in somebody.
To make a hole through something.
To make a hole with, or as if with, a boring instrument; to cut a circular hole by the rotary motion of a tool.
definitions.example: An insect bores into a tree.

definitions.definition: To form or enlarge (something) by means of a boring instrument or apparatus.
definitions.example: to bore a steam cylinder or a gun barrel; to bore a hole

definitions.definition: To make (a passage) by laborious effort, as in boring; to force a narrow and difficult passage through.
definitions.example: to bore one's way through a crowd

definitions.definition: To be pierced or penetrated by an instrument that cuts as it turns.
definitions.example: This timber does not bore well.

definitions.definition: To push forward in a certain direction with laborious effort.
(of a horse) To shoot out the nose or toss it in the air.
To fool; to trick.
A pit or hole which has been bored.
Fragments thrown up when something is bored or drilled.
Any organism that bores into a hard surface
Causing boredom; unable to engage or hold the interest.
definitions.example: What a boring film that was! I almost fell asleep.

definitions.definition: Used, or designed to be used, to drill holes.
definitions.example: boring equipment

definitions.definition: Capable of penetrating; piercing.
(usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.
(physical) Adaptation or adjustment.
(personal) Adaptation or adjustment.
The place where sediments can make, or have made, a sedimentation.
Modifications to make one's way of speaking similar to others involved in a conversation or discourse; code-switching.
To cut unevenly.
To tease.
Unevenly cut; having the texture of something so cut.
Having a rough quality.
Of an array, having a different cardinality in each dimension, such that a representation on paper would appear uneven.
The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them
A specific ritual or procedure associated with such magic; a spell.
The supernatural forces which are drawn on in such a ritual
Something producing successful and remarkable results, especially when not fully understood; an enchanting quality; exceptional skill.
A conjuring trick or illusion performed to give the appearance of supernatural phenomena or powers.
The art or practice of performing conjuring tricks and illusions.
Complicated or esoteric code that is not expected to be generally understood.
To produce, transform (something), (as if) by magic.
Having supernatural talents, properties or qualities attributed to magic.
Producing extraordinary results, as though through the use of magic; wonderful, amazing.
definitions.example: a magic moment

definitions.definition: Pertaining to conjuring tricks or illusions performed for entertainment etc.
definitions.example: a magic show; a magic trick

definitions.definition: Great; excellent.
definitions.example: — I cleaned up the flat while you were out. — Really? Magic!

definitions.definition: Describing the number of nucleons in a particularly stable isotopic nucleus; 2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126, and 184.
Being a literal number or string value with no meaning or context, not defined as a constant or variable
definitions.example: The code is full of magic numbers and we can't figure out what they mean.

definitions.definition: An assessment, such as an annual personnel performance review used as the basis for a salary increase or bonus, or a summary of a particular situation.
A completion of a mathematical operation; a valuation.
Determination of the value of a variable or expression.
The quality or state of being in good health.
The process of learning about and engaging in behaviors that are likely to result in optimal health.
To notice or view, especially carefully or with attention to detail.
definitions.example: From this vantage point we can observe the behavior of the animals in their natural habitat.

definitions.definition: To follow or obey the custom, practice, or rules (especially of a religion).
definitions.example: Please observe all posted speed limits.

definitions.definition: To take note of and celebrate (a holiday or similar occurrence).
To comment on something; to make an observation.
definitions.example: The senator observed that the bill would be detrimental to his constituents.

definitions.definition: A light brown to brownish orange colour.
Of a light brown to brownish orange color.
A sweet, fortified wine which is blended and matured in wood.
A gem, usually made of minerals.
Requiring much physical effort; toilsome.
Mentally difficult; painstaking.
Industrious.
A savory snack.
Tasty, attractive to the palate.
definitions.example: The fine restaurant presented an array of savory dishes; each was delicious.

definitions.definition: Salty and/or spicy, but not sweet.
definitions.example: The mushrooms, meat, bread, rice, peanuts and potatoes were all good savory foods.

definitions.definition: Umami, modern
definitions.example: The savory rabbit soup contrasted well with the sweet cucumber sandwiches with jam.

definitions.definition: Morally or ethically acceptable.
definitions.example: Readers are to be warned that quotations in this chapter contain some not so savory language.

definitions.definition: A garment worn over other clothing to protect it; a coverall or boiler suit. A garment, for manual labor or for casual wear, often made of a single piece of fabric, with long legs and a bib upper, supported from the shoulders with straps, and having several large pockets and loops for carrying tools.
(in the plural) A garment, worn for manual labor, with an integral covering extending to the chest, supported by straps.
All-encompassing, all around.
Generally; with everything considered.
definitions.example: Overall, there is not enough evidence to form a clear conclusion.

definitions.definition: A group of igneous and plutonic rocks composed primarily of feldspar and quartz. Usually contains one or more dark minerals, which may be mica, pyroxene, or amphibole. Granite is quarried for building stone, road gravel, decorative stone, and tombstones. Common colors are gray, white, pink, and yellow-brown.
Toughness; the quality of having a thick skin or being rough.
A small fruit, usually red, black or yellow, with a smooth hard seed and a short hard stem.
Prunus subg. Cerasus, trees or shrubs that bear cherries.
The wood of a cherry tree.
(color) Cherry red.
Virginity, especially female virginity as embodied by a hymen.
A subtree consisting of a node with exactly two leaves.
A cricket ball.
Cherrytop.
Burning tip of a cigarette.
Containing or having the taste of cherries.
Of a bright red colour; cherry red.
(often of cars) In excellent condition; mint condition.
A three-dimensional measure of space that comprises a length, a width and a height. It is measured in units of cubic centimeters in metric, cubic inches or cubic feet in English measurement.
definitions.example: The room is 9x12x8, so its volume is 864 cubic feet.

definitions.definition: Strength of sound; loudness.
definitions.example: Please turn down the volume on the stereo.

definitions.definition: The issues of a periodical over a period of one year.
definitions.example: I looked at this week's copy of the magazine. It was volume 23, issue 45.

definitions.definition: A bound book.
A single book of a publication issued in multi-book format, such as an encyclopedia.
definitions.example: The letter "G" was found in volume 4.

definitions.definition: A roll or scroll, which was the form of ancient books.
Quantity.
definitions.example: The volume of ticket sales decreased this week.

definitions.definition: A rounded mass or convolution.
The total supply of money in circulation or, less frequently, total amount of credit extended, within a specified national market or worldwide.
An accessible storage area with a single file system, typically resident on a single partition of a hard disk.
To be conveyed through the air, waft.
To cause to move through the air, waft.
To swell.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
definitions.example: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

definitions.definition: Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
definitions.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.

definitions.definition: The harness, trappings etc. of a horse, hawk, or other animal.
Fittings, such as handles, of a door, coffin, or other wooden item.
The stock and forearm of a weapon.
The pieces of wood or metal put round pages of type to make proper margins and fill the spaces between the pages and the chase.
Any material on the page other than the text and pictures of stories.
The present time.
definitions.example: Now is the right time.

definitions.definition: (often with "the") The state of not paying attention to the future or the past.
definitions.example: She is living in the now.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in phenomenology) A particular instant in time, as perceived at that instant.
Present; current.
Fashionable; popular; up to date; current.
definitions.example: I think this band's sound is very now.

definitions.definition: At the time the will is written. Used in order to prevent any inheritance from being transferred to a person of a future marriage. Does not indicate the existence of a previous marriage.
definitions.example: Now wife.

definitions.definition: At the present time.
definitions.example: Now I am six.

definitions.definition: (sentence) Used to introduce a point, a qualification of what has previously been said, a remonstration or a rebuke.
definitions.example: Now stop that, Jimmy!

definitions.definition: Differently from the immediate past; differently from a more remote past or a possible future; differently from all other times.
definitions.example: Now I am ready.

definitions.definition: Differently from the situation before a stated event or change of circumstance.
definitions.example: Now all the children have grown up and left, the house is very quiet.

definitions.definition: At the time reached within a narration.
definitions.example: He now asked her whether she had made pudding.

definitions.definition: In the context of urgency.
definitions.example: Now listen, we must do something about this.

definitions.definition: As 'but now': Very recently; not long ago; up to the present.
Since, because, in light of the fact; often with that.
definitions.example: Now that we're all here, let's start the meeting.

definitions.definition: Indicates a signal to begin.
definitions.example: Now! Fire all we've got while the enemy is in reach!

definitions.definition: A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
A physical object whose shape is used as a guide to make other objects.
A generic model or pattern from which other objects are based or derived.
A macromolecule which provides a pattern for the synthesis of another molecule.
A partially defined class or function, that can be instantiated in a variety of ways depending on the instantiation arguments.
To set up or mark off using a template.
To provide a template or pattern for.
An achromatic colour intermediate between black and white.
An animal or thing of grey colour, such as a horse, badger, or salmon.
An extraterrestrial humanoid with grayish skin, bulbous black eyes, and an enlarged head.
A penny with a tail on both sides, used for cheating.
To become gray.
definitions.example: My hair is beginning to gray.

definitions.definition: To cause to become gray.
To turn progressively older, alluding to graying of hair through aging (used in context of the population of a geographic region)
definitions.example: the graying of America

definitions.definition: To give a soft effect to (a photograph) by covering the negative while printing with a ground-glass plate.
Having a color somewhere between white and black, as the ash of an ember.
Dreary, gloomy.
Having an indistinct, disputed or uncertain quality.
Relating to older people.
definitions.example: the gray dollar, i.e. the purchasing power of the elderly

definitions.definition: Cleaning and polishing of an animal's teeth.
A dental sound.
Of or concerning the teeth, as in dental care.
Of or concerning dentistry.
Made with the tongue touching the teeth, as in dental fricative.
A massage of the back.
